config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society works best when individual rights are protected first, because once those rights are weakened, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to abuse power in the name of the common good. In my view, the interests of society matter, but they should be pursued through laws and policies that respect personal dignity and freedom. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting individuals, not by sacrificing them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara mutlak, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu harus menjadi batas yang jelas, bukan sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa tujuannya penting. Tentu saja masyarakat punya kepentingan nyata, seperti keselamatan publik dan kesehatan umum, tetapi itu harus dicapai dengan pembatasan yang sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, kita berisiko menjadikan warga hanya alat demi tujuan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically come before individual rights, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very aware that appeals to “public order” or “the common good” can be used to silence criticism, punish minorities, or excuse heavy-handed state power. Society does have real needs, like safety and social stability, but those goals should be pursued within clear limits that protect dignity and freedom. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the majority can too easily decide whose rights are worth keeping.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu sering “kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan perluasan kekuasaan yang akhirnya merugikan orang biasa. Tapi saya juga tidak setuju kalau hak individu dipahami seolah-olah absolut dalam semua situasi; kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan pencegahan bahaya nyata memang kadang menuntut pembatasan yang sempit dan dapat diuji. Yang saya tolak adalah logika bahwa mayoritas otomatis berhak mengorbankan hak minoritas demi tujuan kolektif. Jadi, bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi pagar utama, sementara kepentingan masyarakat hanya boleh diikuti sejauh tidak merusak pagar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not sit above individual rights as a default rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I am very wary of how easily “public order” or “the common good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or justify excessive state power, so I do not trust majorities to decide whose rights can be sacrificed. At the same time, I do not think rights are absolute in every situation; public health and real safety concerns can justify narrow limits, but only when they are necessary, proportionate, and accountable. Society is strongest when it protects individuals first, because once rights become negotiable, everyone becomes vulnerable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari pengalaman Amerika saya, “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan dan mengorbankan orang biasa. Hak individu memang tidak absolut dalam setiap situasi, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, benar-benar perlu, dan bisa diuji; kalau tidak, mayoritas akan terlalu mudah menindas minoritas atas nama tujuan kolektif. Justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun ketika hak individu menjadi pagar utama, bukan ketika hak itu dianggap sekunder. Kepentingan publik tetap penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menjadikan warga sebagai alat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
